climate.description: Classic Yorkshire summer - expect all four seasons. Windy moorland summits amplify conditions.

fondo_rating.scoring_notes: Migrated from gravel-race-automation. Original score: 79. adventure dimension dropped, road_surface added. Score recalculated: 76.

course_description.character: A loop out of and back to Settle (the HQ moved here from Horton-in-Ribblesdale for 2026) through remote corners of the Yorkshire Dales National Park — moorland, gritstone valleys, and exposed high passes on roads that stay open to normal traffic throughout. Riders start in small groups of no more than 20, which thins the field-atmosphere but also means you're often alone with the terrain. The organizer has had to alter the route repeatedly in recent years for roadworks and winter damage, so the exact course is less stable year to year than most established sportives.
course_description.signature_challenge: Park Rash — sections reported at 22-25% gradient, the course's hardest test. A longer, less brutal bypass via Kidstones Pass (max 13.8%) exists for riders who don't want the direct line.
suffering_zones: Langcliffe Scar: 2.3km at 7.8% average, 18% max — a brutal opener
Malham Tarn and Nab End — early exposed moorland
Park Rash: the intended signature climb at up to 22-25%
Grinton Moor: roughly 3.8km grinding across wild Swaledale moorland
Coal Road: 2.8km at 7.8% average, delivering riders to Dent Station, England's highest mainline railway stop
Birkdale and Mallerstang Common — remote, exposed, with little to no mobile coverage

logistics.lodging_strategy: Settle offers B&Bs, pubs, and camping. Grassington (15min drive) and Skipton (20min) provide more options.
logistics.transport: New HQ at Settle with parking, changing rooms, post-ride food and beer. Multiple feed stations including Moorcock Inn.

history.origin_story: Established as Fred Whitton Challenge alternative. Supports Dave Rayner Fund for young cyclists. Iconic UK sportive status.

biased_opinion.summary: Étape du Dales was created to fund the Dave Rayner Foundation, which sends young British riders racing in continental Europe, and BikeRadar has historically ranked it alongside the Fred Whitton Challenge as one of the 'monuments' of the UK sportive scene. A 2025 BikeRadar assessment even judged its course harder than the Fred Whitton over comparable distance. But the numbers tell a different story than the reputation: BikeRadar reported entries had been 'falling off a cliff,' and the 2026 results page shows roughly 128 timed finishers across both distances — a fraction of the event's historic peak field. The terrain (Park Rash at up to 22-25%, remote exposed moorland, unpredictable road surfaces) is still genuinely brutal. What's changed is the atmosphere around it.
strengths: Genuine charitable purpose — proceeds support the Dave Rayner Foundation's work funding young British riders racing in Europe
Real, unfiltered Yorkshire Dales terrain — Park Rash's 22-25% ramps and remote moorland sections are genuinely severe climbing
A 2025 BikeRadar assessment rated the course harder than the Fred Whitton Challenge over comparable distance and elevation
Small start groups (max 20 riders) reduce bunch chaos on narrow Dales roads
weaknesses: Field size has shrunk dramatically from its 'monument' peak — BikeRadar described entries as 'falling off a cliff,' and the 2026 results show roughly 128 finishers across both distances
Not closed-road — riders share the route with normal traffic throughout

Course instability: Park Rash and Langcliffe Scar were pulled from the route only five days before the 2024 event due to roadworks, and the organizer cited winter road damage when altering the 2026 course
Remote, exposed sections have little to no mobile phone coverage, and DNF riders may need to arrange their own return transport
The organizer's own material is inconsistent about the event's founding — BikeRadar says 2005, the organizer's rider information claims 'over 25 years' by counting an earlier charity-ride lineage
biased_opinion.bottom_line: The terrain still earns the reputation — this is a genuinely hard, honest Yorkshire Dales test, and the Dave Rayner Foundation cause is worth supporting. But go in knowing you'll likely be riding a much smaller field than the event's 'UK monument' reputation implies, on open roads, with a course that can change on short notice.

final_verdict.one_liner: Ultimate Yorkshire Dales sufferfest - 10,500ft of cinematic climbing punishment.
final_verdict.should_you_race: Yes if you want genuinely hard Yorkshire Dales climbing for a real cause and don't need a large-field atmosphere. Skip if you're chasing the event's historic 'UK monument' reputation — the field has shrunk well below that scale.
final_verdict.alternatives: If you want the large-field version of a hard UK sportive: Fred Whitton Challenge (Lake District). If you want similarly remote Dales moorland with a different organizer: Struggle Dales.

citations.label: BikeRadar — History, Reputation and Field-Size Decline
British Cycling — Étape du Dales by the Rayner Foundation

eligibility.notes: Resolved via official site: 2026 edition ran 5 July 2026, HQ at Settle (moved from Horton-in-Ribblesdale) — confirms Settle over the third-party 'Grassington' listing previously flagged as a conflict.
